_comment: Per-(case_id, arch) golden. Empty arch map => bootstrap: the test skips and prints the observed value. DISCRETE golden = exact scalar (== assert). CONTINUOUS golden = object {baseline_ms, cv, n, [k_sigma], [rel_floor]}; gate = baseline_ms*(1+max(k_sigma*cv, rel_floor)), defaults k_sigma=4, rel_floor=0.01. A legacy scalar continuous entry still works via the baseline*1.05 fallback. cv MUST come from a multi-run bootstrap; sm120 = cross-session (5 independent containers), sm90/sm100/sm103 = same-session REPEAT=5 (floor absorbs the gap; refine with cross-session later).

sm120._src: xsession build2800, 5 independent containers: 2.7876/2.7863/2.7884/2.7882/2.7884; cv 0.029% -> 1% floor binds

sm103._src: samesession build2801 REPEAT=5 B300-pcie: 1.0373/1.0363/1.0405/1.0384/1.0415; cv 0.19% -> 1% floor binds

sm120._src: xsession build2800, 5 independent containers: 0.0865/0.0858/0.0856/0.0858/0.0860; cv 0.36% -> k*cv~1.43% gate

sm103._src: samesession build2801 REPEAT=5 B300-pcie: 0.0599/0.0532/0.0532/0.0553/0.0522; cv 5.04% (first val warmup outlier; drop-first cv 2.11%) -> gate ~20%, NEARLY USELESS. INSENSITIVE case: 0.053ms kernel too short for stable CUDA-event timing on fast B300. TODO raise warmup/iters or treat as discrete-only (launch_count=71 still guards structure).
